revision.text:
[[ja:舟形石棺]] [[File:Hodota-kofungun Hachimanzuka-kofun sekishitsu.JPG|thumb|250px|right|'''Sarcophagus from the Hodota Hachimanzuka Kofun''']]'''Funagata Sarcophagus''' (舟形石棺) is a type of {{ill|Coffin|en|Coffin|qid=Q184418|lt=Coffin|1=Coffin}} from the [[Kofun period]]. == Overview == The Funagata Sarcophagus is a variant of hollowed-out [[Sarcophaguss]] with an oval cross-section formed by joining the main body and the lid. Compared to the {{ill|Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|ja|割竹形石棺|1=Waritakegata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split-bamboo-shaped Coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}} (split-bamboo-shaped Sarcophagus), it offers greater stability, with both ends cut diagonally to resemble the shape of a {{ill|Boat|en|Boat|qid=Q35872|lt=Boat|1=Boat}}, hence its name.
It is considered a transformed version of the Waritakegata SarcophagusSugiyama Shinsaku, "Funagata Sarcophagus," in Teruya Esaka et al. (2005), p. 362. and often features rope-holding projections or built-in stone pillows.
The coffins, primarily dated to the mid-4th to early-6th centuries, were constructed using locally sourced stone and served as burial containers for regional leaders. They have been found across Japan, including {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|qid=Q160420|lt=Saga|1=Saga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|qid=Q133879|lt=Fukui|1=Fukui Prefecture}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, and {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}.Nagahara Keiji et al. (1999), p. 1013. == Prominent Examples of Funagata Sarcophagus == * {{ill|Aotsuka_Kofun|en|Aotsuka_Kofun|qid=Q11661131|lt=Aotsuka Kofun|1=Aotsuka Kofun}}: A scallop-shaped kofun from the mid-Kofun period located in [[:en:Kanonji|Kanonji]], {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. Made from welded tuff from Mount Aso, the same material is used for the circular Maruyama Kofun in Domoto, Kanonji. * [[Usuzuka Kofun]]: A keyhole-shaped kofun located in Usuki City, [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]]. Two Funagata Sarcophagus were discovered here, measuring 2.85m and 2.25m in length. * [[Hodota Kofun Cluster|Hodota Kofungun]]: A group of kofun located in Hodota and Ide, [[Takasaki City]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, consisting of three large keyhole-shaped tombs (Futagoyama Kofun, Hachimanzuka Kofun, and Yakushizuka Kofun), each containing Funagata Sarcophagus. == References == == Bibliography == * Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, Shuichi Sakazume, eds., ''New Japanese Archaeological Dictionary,'' New Science Publishing, 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2. * Keiji Nagahara, ed., Eiji Ishigami et al., ''Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary,'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8. == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} * {{ill|Coffin|en|Coffin|qid=Q184418|lt=Coffin|1=Coffin}} * {{ill|Funagata Mokkan|ja|舟形木棺|1=Funagata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Mokkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Mokkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone Coffin Grave|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Coffin Grave|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Wooden Coffin Grave|ja|木棺墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin Grave|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487466}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|舟形石棺|version=78793387|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:舟形石棺]] {{wikidata link|Q11613771}} [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
